items.header: Agents move from demos to daily infrastructure
items.friendlyHeader: AI is becoming part of everyday work
items.summary: Today’s issue is about AI leaving the novelty layer and entering working systems: coding agents inside Slack, models built for long-horizon work, agent discovery standards, safer deployment testing, and practical AI in homes, labs, robots, planning offices, and internal app development.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s issue looks at AI tools that are starting to do real jobs: helping engineers, scientists, planners, robot builders, app teams, and people at home. The big theme is simple: AI is becoming more useful when it fits into normal daily workflows.

items.header: Agent products move from demos to daily operations
items.friendlyHeader: AI agents are starting to do real work
items.summary: Today’s issue tracks a practical shift: new models are stretching long-running work, while agent products are moving into websites, customer service, finance, security, analytics, and software testing. The useful question is less whether agents are coming, and more where guardrails, context, and human review need to sit.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s issue looks at AI tools that can help with websites, customer service, finance, security, coding, research, and voice. The big theme: AI is becoming more useful when it has clear limits, good data, and people checking important decisions.

items.header: Agents move from demos into daily operations
items.friendlyHeader: AI agents are getting ready for real work
items.summary: Today's issue tracks a practical shift in AI: agents are getting safer identities, more persistent workspaces, better voice interfaces, and clearer enterprise adoption paths. The signal is less about spectacle and more about where AI starts changing ordinary workflows, buying decisions, customer support, learning, and local development.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s brief looks at useful AI changes you can actually act on: safer agent access, voice translation, workplace training, customer support automation, and open-source tools. The big theme is AI moving from chat boxes into everyday work systems.

items.header: Agents move from demos to daily workflows
items.friendlyHeader: AI agents are moving into everyday work
items.summary: Today’s brief tracks the practical edge of AI: frontier-model access rules, agent platforms moving into Microsoft 365, WhatsApp, GitHub and Codex, and the trust checks needed when AI-generated work enters professional life.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s issue looks at useful AI changes for work and daily life: smarter assistants, coding tools, business agents, privacy infrastructure, and a reminder to check AI-generated claims before trusting them.

items.header: Agents need safer tools, steadier infrastructure, and better habits
items.friendlyHeader: AI is becoming more practical, but also needs better guardrails
items.summary: Today’s issue tracks the practical side of AI adoption: model access, persistent coding agents, workplace training, live translation, open models, agent security, and infrastructure that makes AI systems more useful in real work. The common thread is less glamour and more operating discipline: if AI is going to shape daily life for the better, it needs usable workflows, safer defaults, and tools people can actually trust.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s brief looks at AI tools people can actually use: coding agents, workplace training, live translation, local models, security tests, and open-source projects. The main theme is simple: useful AI needs good workflows, strong safety checks, and infrastructure that keeps working after the demo ends.

items.header: AI moves deeper into work, security, and personal tools
items.friendlyHeader: AI is becoming part of everyday work
items.summary: Today’s issue focuses on practical AI adoption: enterprise rollouts, agent workspaces, model evaluation, cyber risk, local agents, and the open-source tools shaping how people build with AI. The useful signal is clear: AI is becoming less of a standalone chatbot and more of an operating layer for work, learning, software, and trust.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s brief looks at how AI is showing up in normal work and daily decisions: banking, education, coding, security, live tools, and open-source projects. The main theme is practical AI moving from experiments into systems people actually rely on.

items.header: Agents move from demos to daily infrastructure
items.friendlyHeader: AI agents are becoming everyday tools
items.summary: Today’s issue tracks AI moving into the operating layer of work: persistent coding agents, faster open models, live translation, safer autonomy controls, and practical consumer tools. The through-line is less about spectacle and more about where AI starts reducing friction in everyday decisions, workflows, and creative systems.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s brief looks at AI becoming more useful in normal work and life: coding agents that can keep working, faster models, live voice translation, safer controls, and tools for shopping and music. The big story is practical AI moving from demos into daily routines.

items.header: Agents move from demos into payments, research, coding, and local models
items.friendlyHeader: AI agents are getting closer to real-world jobs.
items.summary: Today’s Daily Intelligence tracks practical AI shifts: agentic commerce gets payment rails, research notebooks gain code execution, voice translation becomes more conversational, and open models/tools keep moving useful workflows closer to everyday hardware and teams.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s issue looks at agents paying for things, research tools doing more work, live translation improving, open models helping coders, and practical tools moving AI closer to normal teams and laptops.

items.header: Frontier models, working agents, and practical AI plumbing
items.friendlyHeader: Big AI tools are moving closer to everyday work.
items.summary: Today's brief tracks a busy AI cycle: a new top-tier Claude release, more live translation from Google, Codex moving deeper into business workflows, and several agent tools that make AI systems easier to build, govern, and use.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s issue covers stronger AI models, live voice translation, coding helpers for more kinds of workers, and practical tools that help people build and control AI agents.

items.header: Agents move from demos into workflows, costs, and risk.
items.friendlyHeader: Agents move from demos into workflows, costs, and risk.
items.summary: Today’s issue tracks the parts of AI that are becoming operational: companies preparing for public markets, agents working directly with files and devices, open models pushing coding forward, security teams racing to keep up with model-assisted abuse, and GitHub projects turning the trend into working tools.
items.friendlySummary: Today’s issue tracks the parts of AI that are becoming operational: companies preparing for public markets, agents working directly with files and devices, open models pushing coding forward, security teams racing to keep up with model-assisted abuse, and GitHub projects turning the trend into working tools.
